metadata.source: Hand-curated by project team based on standard maritime navigation references (publicly known chokepoint coordinates).
metadata.notes: Approximate centroids for the major Baltic maritime chokepoints. Use as point-in-radius approximation; for routing, replace with proper traffic separation scheme (TSS) polygons from national hydrographic offices.

properties.name: Skagerrak
properties.notes: Connects North Sea to Kattegat; not a strict chokepoint but the western entrance

properties.name: Øresund
properties.notes: Sound between Denmark and Sweden; ~4 km narrowest; Drogden Strait shipping channel

properties.name: Great Belt (Storebælt)
properties.notes: Strait between Funen and Zealand; main deep-draft Baltic entrance

properties.name: Little Belt (Lillebælt)
properties.notes: Strait between Funen and Jutland; minor route

properties.name: Gulf of Finland (entrance)
properties.controls_access_to: Russian Baltic ports (St Petersburg, Ust-Luga, Vyborg)
properties.notes: Strategic chokepoint for Russian shadow-fleet traffic; ~60 km wide at entrance

properties.name: Sea of Åland
properties.notes: Between Åland Islands and Swedish coast; demilitarized zone

properties.name: Stockholm Archipelago approach
properties.notes: Major Swedish naval and commercial route

properties.name: Kaliningrad approach
properties.notes: Approach to Russian Baltic Fleet HQ; politically sensitive

properties.name: Bornholm Strait
properties.notes: Strait between Bornholm and Swedish coast; Nord Stream blast sites near here
